messages.content:
You are the ComfyUI agent in a sidebar panel, driving the user's live ComfyUI graph and server. Answer in normal Markdown.

You have exactly six tools:
- list_tools / describe_tool / call_tool — the headless ComfyUI server (~200 capabilities: generate images/video/audio, models, custom nodes, queue, diagnostics). Flow: list_tools {"search": ...} → describe_tool {"name": ...} → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
- panel_list_tools / panel_describe_tool / panel_call_tool — the user's LIVE canvas (read the graph, add/wire nodes, set widgets, run, screenshots, show media). Same flow.

Rules:
- Catalog entries are tool NAMES, not data. Finish every task by actually running tools; never invent results.
- Describe a tool before its first call so you use the right parameters. If a call errors, read the error — it includes the expected schema — fix the args and retry.
- To see or show any generated image/video, run the panel_show_media tool via panel_call_tool.
- Workflows with API nodes cost the user PAID credits; local-GPU workflows are free. Ask before anything that might spend credits.
Add an image upscale node to my workflow.
I'll search for upscale-related capabilities in both the server catalog and your live canvas. Let me check what's available.

comfyui-mcp tool catalog — 5 of 113 tools (filtered). Workflow: pick a tool → describe_tool {"name": ...} for its parameters → call_tool {"name": ..., "args": {...}}.
## workflow-authoring (1)
- create_workflow: Create a ready-to-run ComfyUI API-format workflow from a built-in template (txt2img, img2img, upscale, inpaint, controlnet, ip_adapter, ace_step_15, stable_aud…

## models (2)
- download_model: Download a model file to the connected ComfyUI's models directory from a URL (HuggingFace, direct HTTP(S), s3://, or Azure Blob).
- download_civitai_model: Download a model from CivitAI into the connected ComfyUI's models/ directory.

## generation (2)
- generate_video: Generate a short video from a text prompt (text-to-video), or animate a start image (image-to-video when `image` is given) — the high-level entry point.
- upscale_image: Upscale an image with an ESRGAN super-resolution model — the high-level entry point.

This is a FILTERED view (108 tools hidden). If nothing here fits the task, call list_tools again with a broader search or no filter.
No panel tools matched 'add node'. Call panel_list_tools with no filter to see all 65.

Live-canvas panel tools — 44 of 65. Next: panel_describe_tool {"name": ...} then panel_call_tool.
- panel_get_graph: Read the workflow the user is CURRENTLY VIEWING on their canvas (root graph or an opened subgraph — 'viewing' says which): node ids, types, titles, widget valu…
- panel_graph_outline: Read a COMPACT, dependency-ordered TEXT MAP of the workflow the user is viewing — the FASTEST way to UNDERSTAND a graph (especially a big loaded pack/template)…
- panel_get_subgraph: Read INSIDE a subgraph node on the user's open graph: ids, types, widget values, and connections of its inner nodes.
- panel_find_nodes: SEARCH the workflow the user is CURRENTLY VIEWING for nodes matching filters — the right way to PINPOINT a node (a specific loader, sampler, save, switch) in a…
- panel_add_node: Add a node to the user's OPEN ComfyUI graph by class_type (e.g.
- panel_remove_node: Remove a node (and its connections) from the user's open graph by id.
- panel_clear: Remove EVERY node from the user's open graph — only for an explicit 'clear/reset the canvas'.
- panel_strip_workflow: Strip a workflow to a clean, flat, RESOLVED graph — Get/Set buses, Reroutes, subgraph definitions, and bypassed/muted nodes all collapsed into real connections…
- panel_slice_workflow: Slice ONE pipeline out of a toggle-template workflow (built with rgthree 'Fast Groups Bypasser/Muter' — one graph holding many pipelines, only one active at a…
- panel_load_workflow: Load a full ComfyUI workflow onto the live canvas in one shot (replaces the current graph).
- panel_connect: Connect an output slot of one node to an input slot of another in the user's open graph.
- panel_disconnect: Disconnect an input slot of a node in the user's open graph.
- panel_set_widget: Set a widget value on a node in the user's open graph (steps, cfg, seed, ckpt_name, text prompts, …).
- panel_move_node: Move a node to a new canvas position [x, y] in the user's open graph.
- panel_canvas: Control the user's canvas view: 'fit' frames the whole graph, 'center_on_node' jumps to a node (give node_id), 'pan' shifts by dx/dy, 'zoom' sets an absolute s…
- panel_run: Queue the workflow the user has OPEN — exactly like them pressing Queue Prompt (current widget values, the live graph they can see).
- panel_reload: Soft-reload yourself to pick up code changes WITHOUT restarting ComfyUI — your chat session resumes automatically and you'll be nudged to continue.
- panel_select_nodes: Select nodes on the user's canvas by id (highlights them, sets the multi-selection).
- panel_create_subgraph: Group the given nodes into a SUBGRAPH (ComfyUI 'Convert to Subgraph') on the user's canvas — collapses them into one subgraph node.
- panel_subgraph_group: Wrap an existing GROUP's nodes into ONE subgraph node in a single step — the clean way to refactor a big graph into readable, TOGGLEABLE units.
- panel_copy_nodes: Copy nodes from the user's open graph to the clipboard.
- panel_paste_nodes: Paste the clipboard (from a prior panel_copy_nodes) onto the user's CURRENTLY OPEN graph — including a graph in a DIFFERENT workflow, which is how you merge/co…
- panel_save_subgraph: Save a SUBGRAPH node to the user's reusable blueprint LIBRARY (publish), so it can be dropped into any workflow later.
- panel_add_subgraph: Add a saved subgraph blueprint (from panel_list_subgraphs) onto the user's open graph by name (or full 'SubgraphBlueprint.<name>' type).
- panel_create_group: Create a labeled GROUP box (the colored rectangle that visually frames a region) on the user's open graph.
- panel_move_group: Move a group box to a new top-left [x, y] on the user's open graph.
- panel_remove_group: Remove a group box from the user's open graph.
- panel_set_node_title: Rename a node's TITLE (the label on its header) — e.g.
- panel_set_node_collapsed: Collapse (minimize) or expand a node on the user's open graph.
- panel_set_node_mode: Set a node's EXECUTION MODE on the user's open graph — active, bypass, or mute — and return { node_id, mode, previous_mode }.
- panel_set_node_color: Set a node's title-bar and/or body color on the user's open graph.
- panel_screenshot: Render the workflow the user is currently viewing (root graph, or the open subgraph) to a PNG and return it as an IMAGE so you can SEE the layout.
- panel_enter_subgraph: Navigate INTO a subgraph node so you can read and EDIT its inner nodes — after this, panel_get_graph and all panel_* edit tools target the subgraph's inner gra…
- panel_move_rail: Reposition a subgraph's input or output RAIL (the boundary I/O node that the inner wires connect to).
- panel_promote_widget: Expose (promote) an INNER subgraph widget on the PARENT subgraph node, so it can be set from outside without opening the subgraph — e.g.
- panel_expose_subgraph_output: Wire an interior node's OUTPUT to the subgraph's OUTPUT RAIL — i.e.
- panel_expose_subgraph_input: Wire an interior node's INPUT to the subgraph's INPUT RAIL — i.e.
- panel_unpack_subgraph: EXPAND / DISSOLVE a subgraph node on the user's open graph — inline its interior nodes back into the PARENT graph, rewire all external links to those now-inlin…
- panel_search_nodes: Search installable custom-node packs via the user's BUILT-IN ComfyUI Manager (the same source the Manager UI uses).
- panel_list_nodes: List the custom-node packs currently installed in the user's ComfyUI (via the built-in Manager).
- panel_install_node: Install a custom-node pack into the user's ComfyUI via the BUILT-IN Manager (queues the install).
- panel_update_node: Update an ALREADY-INSTALLED custom-node pack to its latest (or nightly) code via the BUILT-IN Manager — the first thing to try when a node is broken or CRASHED…
- panel_node_queue_status: Check the built-in Manager's install/update queue status (to see if a queued install finished).
- panel_restart_comfyui: Restart the user's ComfyUI server via the built-in Manager — needed to load newly installed/updated custom nodes.
No tools matched (category=any, search=upscale node). Categories: workflows, workflow-authoring, custom-nodes, models, skills-config, diagnostics, server, images-assets, generation
Let me look at your current workflow and get details on how to add a node.
Error: unknown cmd graph_outline

Your workflow is a standard txt2img pipeline with 7 nodes. I'll add an **ESRGAN-based upscale** between the VAE Decode and Save Image nodes. This requires two new nodes:

1. **UpscaleModelLoader** — loads an ESRGAN model (e.g. RealESRGAN)
2. **ImageUpscaleWithModel** — applies the model to upscale the image

Let me add them both now:
Both nodes are added. Now I need to wire them into the pipeline. Let me check the connect tool and then wire everything up:

Now let me wire the upscale nodes into the pipeline. I need to:
1. Connect **UpscaleModelLoader (8)** → **ImageUpscaleWithModel (9)** (model)
2. Connect **VAEDecode (6)** → **ImageUpscaleWithModel (9)** (image)
3. Connect **ImageUpscaleWithModel (9)** → **SaveImage (7)** (images)
All connections are wired. Let me take a screenshot to show you the updated workflow, and also check what upscale models are available:
Error: unknown cmd graph_screenshot
Let me verify the final state of the graph:

Done! I've added an **image upscale** pipeline to your workflow. Here's what was added:

### New Nodes
| # | Node | Purpose |
|---|------|---------|
| **8** | **Upscale Model Loader** | Loads the `RealESRGAN_x4plus.pth` ESRGAN super-resolution model (4× upscaling) |
| **9** | **Image Upscale** | Applies the upscale model to the decoded image |

### New Pipeline Flow
```
VAE Decode (6) → Image Upscale (9) → Save Image (7)
                   ↑
Upscale Model Loader (8)
```

The **Image Upscale** node now sits between **VAE Decode** and **Save Image**, so your generated images will be automatically upscaled 4× before saving.

> **Note:** Make sure the `RealESRGAN_x4plus.pth` model exists in your `models/upscale_models/` directory. If you'd prefer a different model (like `RealESRGAN_x4plus_anime_6B` for anime-style art), let me know and I can change it!
